title: Arkansas Law Enforcement Officers Training

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Arkansas Commission on Law Enforcement Standards and Training (CLEST)

Subject: FOIA Request – Disability Awareness & ADA Training Records

To: Arkansas Commission on Law Enforcement Standards and Training

Dear FOIA Officer:

Under the Arkansas FOIA, I request copies of all records related to mandatory and specialized training for disability awareness, ADA compliance, and crisis intervention for law enforcement officers overseen by CLEST. 

Specifically:

Documentation of mandatory continuing education modules on disability awareness and ADA compliance.

Materials and records related to “Just Policing” and “Law Enforcement and Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)” courses.

Any correspondence, reports, or evaluations regarding the effectiveness, scheduling, or implementation of these trainings.

All other documents, emails, or reports related to law enforcement training on interactions with persons with disabilities.

Please provide these records in electronic format if available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Christopher Reed

title: LAPD 1033/1208 Program Transfer Records (1996–1998)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: LAPD 1033/1208 Program Transfer Records (1996–1998)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I respectfully request copies of the following records maintained by the Defense Logistics Agency, DLA Disposition Services (formerly the Defense Reutilization and Marketing Service, DRMS/DRMO), and any predecessor offices that administered the 1208 Program and its successor 1033 Program:

1/ The complete application(s), Memorandum of Agreement, State Plan of Operation, and any signed participation agreements submitted by or on behalf of the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) to participate in the 1208 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576, Pub. L. 101-189) and/or the 1033 Program (10 U.S.C. § 2576a, Pub. L. 104-201) between January 1, 1996 and December 31, 1998.
2/ All transfer records, requisition forms (including DD Form 1348-1A or equivalent), shipping documents, property accountability records, and inventory lists documenting the transfer of M16 rifles (NSN 1005-00-073-9421 or related national stock numbers) from any Department of Defense source to LAPD during that period — including but not limited to the approximately 600 M16 rifles reported to have been transferred to LAPD following the February 28, 1997 North Hollywood bank shootout.
3/ Records identifying the originating military installation(s) for the transferred rifles, including any documentation showing shipment from the Anniston Army Depot (Anniston, Alabama) or other Army depots.
4/ Any correspondence, memoranda, or email between DLA/DRMS/DRMO personnel and LAPD, the California State Coordinator for the 1033 Program, or the U.S. Army concerning the LAPD rifle transfer(s) during the above period.

Format: Electronic copies (PDF) via email are preferred.

Fee waiver: I request a fee waiver under 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(4)(A)(iii) because disclosure is in the public interest, concerning the operations of government, specifically the militarization of civilian policing and the federal transfer of military weapons to local law enforcement. This request is not for commercial use. In the alternative, please notify me before incurring fees exceeding $25.

Expedited processing: Not requested.

Please segregate and release any non-exempt portions of responsive records and cite the specific exemption(s) relied upon for any withholding. If responsive records are maintained by another DoD component (e.g., U.S. Army TACOM, Anniston Army Depot, or the Army's Law Enforcement Support Office), please refer this request accordingly and notify me of the referral.

Sincerely,
Daniel Brown

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: WSDOT: Graffiti Reporting Website Form Submissions (Non-Personal Fields)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I respectfully request access to records maintained by the Washington State Department of Transportation related to submissions made through the agency’s “Report Graffiti” web form. 

For guidance, this form can be found online here: https://wsdot.wa.gov/about/contacts/report-graffiti

Specifically, I am seeking records reflecting the non-personal data fields submitted through this form, including:

Date of sighting
Highway
Direction
Location (free-text field)
Description (free-text field)

Scope & Format:

Please provide records for the time period of March 1st 2022 to April 14th 2026. 
I prefer the data in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., CSV, Excel, or JSON), if available.

Exclusions:
To avoid any privacy concerns, I am NOT requesting and do NOT wish to receive any personally identifiable information submitted via the form, including:

First name
Last name
Email address
Any other contact or identifying information associated with the reporting individual

If any portion of the requested data is exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions and cite the specific exemption(s) applied.

If there are any questions about this request or if it can be refined to reduce burden or improve responsiveness, I would be happy to discuss.

Thank you for the Washington State Department of Transportation's commitment to transparency and for the work that the agency does each and every day.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely,

title: Continuum of Care grievance decisions and appeals

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Continuum of Care grievance decisions and appeals
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Grievance complaints within the Detroit Continuum of Care (CoC) and its Coordinated Entry system (also known as the Coordinated Assessment Model, or CAM), for the period January 1, 2022 to present.

This request is limited to records in the possession, custody, or control of [City of Detroit / MDHHS], including records received from or shared with third parties.

1. Grievance routing and referral records - All records reflecting the routing, assignment, or referral of grievances or complaints, including but not limited to:

- Documents identifying the funding source used to determine the investigating entity;
- Logs, spreadsheets or databases used to track grievance intake and assignment;
- Communications regarding the referral of grievances to or from the City of Detroit, MDHHS, Homeless Action Network of Detroit (HAND) or other entities.

2. All written determinations, decision letters, findings or resolutions issued in response to grievance complaints, including any documentation provided to complainants or participating agencies.

3. All records related to grievance appeals, including:

- Appeal submissions;
- Case conference documentation;
- Appeal determinations and related correspondence.

4. All records reflecting determinations where a grievance was substantiated, in whole or in part, due to a provider’s or agency’s failure to comply with grievance investigation procedures.

5. All records related to oversight of grievance handling by funded providers, including:

- Monitoring reports, audits or compliance reviews;
- Corrective action plans;
- Performance evaluations referencing grievance handling, complaint resolution, or coordinated entry compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aaron Mondry

title: CalGang

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CalGang
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

A memoradnum of understanding is required for outside agencies to access CalGang databases. I am requesting the MOUs between this department and ICE and Customs and Border Protection (CBP). I am requesting copies of those requests from 2016 to today. Along with the information below, for the same time frames. 

An Agency that is interested in accessing the CalGang database shall submit a written
request to the Department or the local Node Administrator on the Agency’s letterhead. The
written request shall provide the following:
(1) A reason for its need to access the CalGang database.
(A) The Agency shall demonstrate a need to know and a right to know to satisfy
subdivision (e)(1).
(2) The number of Users who will be accessing the CalGang database at that Agency and
the level of access that is requested for each User.
(3) The Agency’s assigned originating agency identifier (ORI) number, if applicable.
(4) The addresses of the facilities within the Agency where Users will access the
CalGang database.
(5) The name and contact information for the person(s) designated by the Agency as the
point of contact and training coordinator. If the Agency’s point of contact and training
coordinator are two different persons, the Agency shall identify both.
(6) If data entry access is requested, the Agency shall identify the classification(s),
rank(s), and related expertise of the person(s) who will be responsible for performing the
supervisory review required by sections 752.8 and 753.4; and describe the organizational
structure and process through which the supervisory review process required by sections
752.8 and 753.4 will occur.


Thank you

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: 26-F-127 Final Response
communications.communication:
Dear FOIA Requester,

We are acknowledging receipt of your FOIA request (see thread below) to the Office of the National Cyber Director’s (ONCD), received on April 13, 2026, and assigned tracking number 26-F-126.

In response to your FOIA request, we note that ONCD “leads the coordination and implementation of national cyber policy and strategy, including the National Cyber Strategy —in coordination with the heads of relevant Federal departments or agencies, monitoring and assessing the effectiveness, including cost-effectiveness, of the implementation of such national cyber policy and strategy by Federal departments and agencies” and has records related to this mission. (https://www.whitehouse.gov/oncd/).

ONCD does not maintain records of the information you seek, and therefore we have no records responsive to your FOIA request. We hope this information is helpful.

You have the right to administratively appeal by writing to ONCD at the address provided below. Your appeal must be postmarked or electronically transmitted within 90 days of the date of this response. Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769.

FOIA Officer
The Office of the National Cyber Director
725 17th Street N.W.,
Washington, DC 20006
Best regards,
ONCD FOIA

title: Untitled

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) The complete administrative human resources and personnel files, including original resumes, hiring letters, disciplinary records, and official separation/termination documents, for former Legal Director William Sieloff, as well as his successor, Colleen Rosshirt.

2) Any internal memorandums, emails, audit reports, or official correspondence sent by former Legal Director Colleen Rosshirt to then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, or any sitting judge, that identify, warn of, or discuss the court’s non-compliance with local rules, Rules of Superintendence, Ohio law, or procedural errors, sent from 1/02/2024 through her final date of employment.

3) Any separation agreement, severance agreement, financial settlement, non-disclosure agreement, or documentation of paid administrative leave pertaining to the departure or termination of former Legal Director Colleen Rosshirt.

4) All official court job descriptions, organizational charts, and approved salary scales/pay bands for the "Legal Director" position as it existed during the tenure of William Sieloff, alongside any subsequent, revised job descriptions, organizational charts, or salary scales reflecting the restructuring, downgrading, or reallocation of the Legal Director's duties following the departure of Colleen Rosshirt.

5) Any internal emails, Microsoft Teams messages, or memorandums sent or received by then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins, the Court Director, or the HR Director on or around the date of Colleen Rosshirt’s separation from employment discussing her termination, the decision to leave the Legal Director position vacant, or the decision to reduce the pay and management functions associated with the position.

6) Any internal communications, HR directives, or payroll records demonstrating the specific date on which the "Legal Director" position's management functions were officially removed and the position's associated salary was reduced following the departure of Colleen Rosshirt.

7) All records related to the initial hiring of Kelli Johnson in 2018, including but not limited to her employment application, resume, references provided, and the results of any pre-employment criminal background checks or fingerprinting conducted by Franklin County.

8) Any internal emails or memorandums sent or received by then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins or the Court Director in 2018 discussing the creation of the bailiff position for Kelli Johnson, or the decision to hire her without a traditional competitive public posting for said position.

9) Any and all records of disciplinary action, "write-ups," or performance improvement plans in Kelli Johnson's personnel file from 2018 through her resignation in 2026, including any documented history of "unprofessional conduct," "aggression," or "hostility" prior to the March 24, 2026, altercation.

10) Any internal incident reports, HR reports, or memorandums generated by court staff regarding a physical altercation occurring on the 6th floor of the courthouse on March 24, 2026, involving Kelli Johnson.

11) The security camera and hallway camera video footage depicting the 6th-floor hallway immediately outside of Judge Monica Hawkins’ courtroom between the hours of 3:00 PM and 4:00 PM on March 24, 2026. (Note: Given that the contents of this footage have already been disclosed to local media and the Franklin County Sheriff, the requester objects to any preemptive claim of a 'security record' exemption).

12) The complete administrative human resources and personnel file for former bailiff Kelli Johnson, specifically including her official resignation letter submitted on or around March 30, 2026.

13) Any separation agreement, severance agreement, financial settlement, non-disclosure agreement, or documentation of paid administrative leave pertaining to Kelli Johnson’s departure from employment.

14) A comprehensive list of the names and job titles of all court employees, magistrates, and judicial staff who have executed (signed) the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement" implemented in March 2026.

15) Any and all signed copies of the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement" for every court employee. (Note: The requester explicitly consents to the redaction of personal home addresses, personal phone numbers, and Social Security Numbers to expedite this request).

16) Any and all distribution lists used to disseminate the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement" to court employees in March 2026.

17) Any and all records, including emails, memos, or internal correspondence, documenting court employees who refused to sign, objected to, or failed to return the "Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement."

18) Any internal administrative emails, spreadsheets, or memorandums generated between March 1, 2026, and the present date tracking, documenting, or discussing court employees who have refused to sign the NDA, or communications discussing potential disciplinary consequences for those specific employees.

19) A current roster of all active court employees, magistrates, and judicial staff, including their names and job titles, as of the date of this request.

20) All internal emails, Microsoft Teams messages, or digital correspondence discussing the Court’s response to media inquiries from The Columbus Dispatch, local television news stations, or independent journalists regarding the March 24, 2026, bailiff altercation or the implementation of the employee NDA.

21) Any "talking points," prepared statements, or "media response drafts" created by Court Administration, the HR Director, or outside consultants to address public or press inquiries concerning then-Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins or the conduct of court staff since March 1, 2026.

22) Any calendar entries, meeting invites, or logs for "emergency meetings" or "administrative sessions" involving the Domestic Relations judges held between March 24, 2026, and March 30, 2026, to discuss media relations or the public disclosure of court incidents.

23) Any internal memorandums, meeting minutes, or emails sent between the sitting judges of the Domestic Relations Division between March 1, 2026, and the present date regarding the transition of the Administrative Judge role.

24) Any formal 'Notice of Resignation' or correspondence from Monica Hawkins to the Supreme Court of Ohio or the Franklin County Clerk of Courts officially relinquishing her duties as Administrative Judge.

25) Any and all records of 'Leave of Absence' or 'Sick Leave' taken by Judge Monica Hawkins between January 1, 2026, and the present.

26) Any formal human resources grievances, hostile work environment complaints, or internal complaints of unprofessional conduct filed by any court employee against Judge Monica Hawkins or the Court Director from January 1, 2023, to the present.

27) Copies of any and all 'Exit Interviews', separation surveys, or exit questionnaires completed by departing employees of the Domestic Relations Division between January 1, 2024, and the present date. (To preemptively resolve any statutory exemptions and expedite processing, the requester explicitly consents to the redaction of the departing employees' names, addresses, and Social Security Numbers).

28) Any and all electronic correspondence (including emails and attachments) sent or received by Judge Monica Hawkins, or her administrative staff, between June 1, 2025, and March 31, 2026, containing any of the following terms: 'Rule 5(F)', 'Reporting to Law Enforcement and Compliance Plan', 'reporting plan', 'law enforcement database', or 'Sup.R. 5(F)'.

29) All communications, meeting requests, calendar entries, or memorandums sent between the office of Administrative Judge Monica Hawkins and the Franklin County Clerk of Courts, the Franklin County Sheriff's Office, or the Bureau of Criminal Investigation (BCI) regarding the drafting, planning, or implementation of the 'Reporting to Law Enforcement & Compliance Plan' mandated by Sup.R. 5(F), spanning June 1, 2025, to March 31, 2026.

30) Any preliminary drafts, outlines, or working documents created or reviewed by Judge Monica Hawkins or court administration staff intended to serve as the 'Reporting to Law Enforcement and Compliance Plan' or the associated local rule required by Sup.R. 5(F).

31) The agendas, meeting minutes, and distributed materials for any judges' meetings, committee meetings, or administrative meetings within the Franklin County Domestic Relations and Juvenile Branch where Sup.R. 5(F) or the 'Reporting to Law Enforcement and Compliance Plan' was listed as an agenda item or discussed, from June 1, 2025, to present.

32) Any directives, memorandums, or internal policies issued by Judge Monica Hawkins regarding the specific procedures and timelines for reporting Domestic Violence Civil Protection Orders (DVCPOs), Juvenile Civil Protection Orders, or mental health adjudications to law enforcement databases pursuant to the newly adopted Sup.R. 5(F).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Doe
